textByHashes.23a8ec0c4af830bb34cc734703e75d3851141cb6: Hello Adventurers. Welcome to Mill Lane Mill. Can I help you?
textByHashes.4ac439aece4d547698129c10a7f5df21395f2e02: This is Mill Lane Mill. Millers of the finest flour in Gielinor, and home to the Miller family for many generations
textByHashes.8ae195eba1efb9a212d4361fa168c220e9780b5c: We take grain from the field nearby and mill into flour.
textByHashes.c6bcff9422aab09200c1a6aa6379fcf7c57f1060: It's the stuff we get from milling the grain. We use it to make bread and cakes and things.
textByHashes.0a963005e16a20a0b09d96028a0e6fe312c4abe1: Hello Adventurer. Welcome to Mill Lane Mill. Can I help you?
textByHashes.99409a147b29e79c470afe6a440ffbba1f095969: I'm Miss Millicent Miller the Miller of Mill Lane Mill. Our family have been milling flour for generations.
textByHashes.938e67a22249cac8e4fa0c43c758c300f36a340a: Making flour is pretty easy. First of all you need to get some grain. You can pick some from wheat fields. There is one just outside the Mill, but there are many others scattered across Gielinor. Feel free to pick wheat
textByHashes.d0f0ede443b48ef078310c8e44910be9941fb4ea: from our field! There always seems to be plenty of wheat there.
textByHashes.3b35458a5999920fe312953e70e708a1ba2b52f0: Yes, or one of the other mills in Gielinor. They all work the same way. Just take your grain to the top floor of the mill (up two ladders, there are three floors including this one) and then place some grain into the hopper.
textByHashes.0f26e6dff8f0dcf79cf2affb3e4f250bf45c49de: Then you need to start the grinding process by pulling the hopper lever. You can add more grain, but each time you add grain you have to pull the hopper lever again.
textByHashes.0b2d21851c0699ade5d506e50e56d96e24022073: The flour appears in this room here, you'll need a pot to put the flour into. One pot will hold the flour made by one load of grain
textByHashes.124cbd99ec662f9ee2d4ca8789b94b6544ffc5f8: And that's it! You now have some pots of finely ground flour of the highest quality. Ideal for making tasty cakes or delicous bread. I'm not a cook so you'll have to ask a cook to find out how to bake things.
